original_hypothesis.title: VotingEscrow checkpoint coverage gap: 255-week loop may fail to fully process 5-year (≈260-week) locks after long inactivity, breaking supply/voting calculations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: VotingEscrow._checkpoint and _supplyAt iterate at most 255 weekly steps, but LOCKTIME is 1825 days (~260.7 weeks). If the contract is inactive for more than 255 weeks, slope changes for the remaining weeks may not be processed, yielding incorrect pointHistory, totalSupply, and balance calculations used for governance weightings.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrated state machine correctness issue (iteration bound < theoretical maximum). While exploitation requires prolonged inactivity, the protocol documentation explicitly relies on periodic checkpointing; if that assumption fails, governance and reward direction can be computed incorrectly.

proof_of_signal.lines: _checkpoint and _supplyAt both: for (uint256 i = 0; i < 255; i++)
proof_of_signal.pattern: iteration cap (255 weeks) smaller than lock duration (~260 weeks)

preconditions: No one calls checkpoint/createLock/increaseAmount/withdraw (anything that triggers _checkpoint) for >255 weeks
There exist slopeChanges scheduled beyond the processed 255-week window

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: bounded iteration insufficient for worst-case time horizon
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: pointHistory not fully updated to current timestamp; supply/votes drift
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no mechanism to continue processing beyond 255 weeks across multiple calls

analysis_questions: Can multiple successive checkpoint() calls advance history further, or is progress stuck due to how epoch/globalEpoch is advanced?
Do any protocol components depend on accurate totalSupplyAt/balanceOfAt across this inactivity window?
validation_requirements: Simulate chain time jump >255 weeks and execute checkpoint() repeatedly to see if it catches up
Compare computed totalSupply() vs expected from locked balances after long inactivity

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After prolonged inactivity, an attacker or any user calls checkpoint(), producing incorrect global state. Subsequent governance decisions or gauge weighting that relies on those calculations may be skewed until corrected (if correction is even possible).
prerequisites: Multi-year inactivity / missed checkpoint assumption failure

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Incorrect voting power/supply can misdirect emissions and governance outcomes
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All veCANTO holders; all markets depending on gauge outcomes
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Potentially recoverable if repeated calls can catch up; otherwise requires redeploy

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No long-horizon time-jump tests validating checkpoint convergence across >255 weeks.

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: divergence between computed supply and sum of user voting power

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
